title: Health Claims Analytics at National Scale
headlineResult: Pricing, live claims dashboards and fraud detection for a group health scheme covering ~800 million people

children.text: Collaboration with a Property & Casualty insurer in India on a state-level group health insurance scheme covering approximately 800 million people — one of the largest such schemes in the world. The goal: a pricing methodology, real-time claims analytics, fraud detection, and insights to optimise reinsurance capacity.

Claims analytics in Python and R across 200,000+ records, an RShiny dashboard for monthly claims tracking, and fraud-indicator analysis tuned to COVID-era claim patterns. Roughly two person-years of consulting effort.
The dashboard improved claims-management efficiency by ~20%; fraud-pattern analysis during COVID-19 contributed to a ~5% reduction in fraudulent claims paid.

title: Motor Insurance Fraud Classifier
headlineResult: PhD research: an ML model that scores the probability of fraud in motor insurance claims

children.text: Doctoral research focused on constructing a robust fraud-detection model for insurance claims — a machine-learning solution that lets users assess the probability of fraud in motor insurance claims.

Combined actuarial validation practices with ML classifiers (CART, gradient boosting, neural networks) and techniques for severely imbalanced data, keeping model interpretability a first-class requirement throughout.
The model improved accuracy and efficiency of fraud-detection processes in the motor insurance domain, and seeded a series of peer-reviewed publications (2019–2024).

title: ML with Business-Rule Triggers for Health Claims Fraud
headlineResult: Combining ML models with business-rule triggers enhanced fraud-detection performance across every model tested

children.text: Health insurance fraud costs the industry billions annually. Research engagement with the Casualty Actuarial Society community proposing a hybrid approach: machine-learning models integrated with business-rule triggers to flag unusual claim patterns for investigation.
Benchmarked ML models with and without rule triggers on claims data, measuring the lift the hybrid design delivers over either technique alone.
Incorporating business-rule triggers greatly enhanced performance across all models evaluated.

title: Crop Revenue Protection Hedging
headlineResult: Hedging mechanism protecting farmer revenue from price fluctuations, built for a Government of India department

children.text: Engagement with a department within the Government of India to design a hedging mechanism that protects farmers' revenue against crop price fluctuations. Roughly two person-years of effort.

Automated daily yield-data collection through Python web scraping (3M+ data points) and built ARIMA models to project crop yields, plus a futures-tracking dashboard covering 12 scenarios across six crops.
Data processing time cut by ~90%; three-month yield forecasts materially improved; manual futures tracking reduced by ~70%.

title: Cancer Pool Pricing
headlineResult: Priced four cancer indemnity packages across 28 Indian states, with premium, frequency and severity dashboards

children.text: Development of a medical indemnity insurance solution tailored for the Indian middle class, analysing cancer types, incidence rates and treatment costs to determine an optimal pricing strategy.
Priced four distinct product packages across 28 states and built a dashboard comparing premium, frequency and severity across 100+ scenarios, by state and age group.
A pricing view that balances affordability for policyholders with effective risk management across geographies and demographics.

title: Using Interpretable Machine Learning Methods: An Application to Health Insurance Fraud Detection
headlineResult: Society of Actuaries Research Institute report: an interpretability framework for health-insurance fraud models

children.text: Establishes a foundational framework for interpretable machine-learning techniques in health-insurance fraud detection. ML algorithms excel at intricate pattern models, but overfitting risk demands rigorous testing — and interpretability remains the pronounced challenge this report addresses.

title: Application of CART-Based Modeling in Motor Insurance Fraud
headlineResult: Classification and Regression Trees applied to motor-claim fraud — a Taylor & Francis chapter

children.text: Applies Classification and Regression Trees (CART) to detecting fraudulent claims in the motor insurance sector; published as a book chapter by Taylor & Francis.

title: A Comparative Study of ML and Deep-Learning Fraud Detection Models for Universal Health Coverage
headlineResult: ML and deep-learning fraud models compared head-to-head for universal health coverage schemes (IJETT)

children.text: Comparative analysis of machine-learning and deep-learning models for identifying fraudulent activity within universal health coverage programmes.

title: TGANs with ML Models in Automobile Insurance Fraud Detection
headlineResult: Tabular GAN augmentation benchmarked against other data-imbalance techniques for auto-claim fraud (IJRTE)

children.text: Investigates Tabular Generative Adversarial Networks (TGANs) paired with ML models for automobile insurance fraud, comparing their effectiveness against established techniques for handling imbalanced data.

title: Correlation and Regression Models for Health Insurance Fraud in the COVID-19 Environment
headlineResult: Actuarial + data-science regression models for detecting health-claim fraud during the pandemic (IJRTE)

children.text: Applies correlation and regression modelling — combining actuarial and data-science techniques — to detect health insurance fraud in the COVID-19 environment.

title: A Predictive Model for Fraud Detection in Motor Insurance using Gradient Boosting
headlineResult: Gradient-boosting fraud model validated against traditional actuarial models — published on IEEE Xplore

children.text: Development and validation of a gradient-boosting predictive model for motor insurance fraud detection, benchmarked against traditional actuarial models.

title: A Proposed Model for Measuring Protection of Policyholders' Interest at Industry Level
headlineResult: An industry-level relativity index for policyholder protection, published in IRDAI's journal

children.text: Proposes a model measuring protection of policyholders' interest by calculating a relativity coefficient for each quantitative measure at industry level.

title: Integrating Actuarial Models with Neural Networks for Automobile Fraud Detection
headlineResult: Traditional actuarial models fused with neural networks for stronger automobile fraud detection (JETIR)

children.text: Explores integrating traditional actuarial models with neural networks to create enhanced fraud-detection systems for automobile insurance.

title: Facilitating the Decision to Implement New Technology in Insurance Organizations
headlineResult: A method and use case for deciding when new technology improves insurance operational efficiency (JETIR)

children.text: Presents a methodology and case study for insurance companies evaluating new-technology implementation aimed at improving operational efficiency.

title: A Framework for Comprehensive Fraud Management using Actuarial Techniques
headlineResult: A comprehensive fraud-management framework built on actuarial techniques (IJSER)

children.text: Outlines a comprehensive framework leveraging actuarial techniques for effective fraud management within insurance operations.
